summary: Reviewed the pre-implementation spec and included source through failure-path scenarios. The main risk is replacing timing literals instead of resolving the active per-session reveal config, which can break existing 40s sessions and challenge-song displays. The host table change is mostly straightforward but needs a couple of workflow safeguards around disambiguation and async brand changes.
appears_sound: Reveal phase calculation sanitizes invalid progress and compares thresholds deterministically in `lib/live/reveal.ts:5-16`.
Auto-advance has an idempotency guard so repeated polling at or after the threshold only triggers once per track in `lib/live/reveal.ts:31-39`.
The session model stores `revealConfig` per live session, which is the right foundation for preserving existing 40s sessions in `lib/live/types.ts:50-58`.

findings.description: Failure sequence: an existing session with stored `revealConfig.nextMs = 40_000` is reopened after the release; the host can advance at 40s while the guest screen advertises `Next song at 60s`, so the live display changes songs 20s before the advertised threshold. Any imported or custom per-session config has the same issue because the proposed change swaps one literal for another.
findings.evidence: The spec says existing sessions keep their stored config, but guest rendering currently uses a hardcoded non-challenge value at `app/guest/[sessionId]/page.tsx:354` and the plan is to replace `30_000` with `60_000` instead of reading `session.revealConfig.nextMs`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: If implementation changes this to derive from `session.revealConfig.nextMs` rather than following the literal replacement, the failure does not occur.
findings.what_would_confirm: A regression test or manual run with a saved 40s session should show the guest threshold remains 40s while new sessions show 60s.

findings.description: Failure sequence: a challenge song starts; reveal state follows `CHALLENGE_REVEAL_CONFIG` at 10/20/25s, but the proposed global placeholder replacement advertises 15/30/40s. Players see album/title/artist appear earlier than the screen says, which is a visible break in the challenge flow.
findings.evidence: The unchanged challenge config is 10/20/25/90 at `lib/live/types.ts:21-26`, while the guest placeholder strings are one unconditioned render path at `app/guest/[sessionId]/page.tsx:317`, `:330`, and `:340`.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: If the implementation branches placeholder text on `runtime.isChallengeSong` or a resolved active reveal config, the spec concern is resolved.
findings.what_would_confirm: Run a challenge-song UI test and assert placeholder times use 10/20/25 while non-challenge uses 15/30/40 or the session config.

findings.description: Failure sequence: a legacy or imported host-controller path lacks `session.revealConfig`; the planned fallback change silently makes it a 60s session, while the spec claims existing sessions keep saved behavior. Static 15/30/40 host badge labels can also misrepresent stored 40s or custom configs.
findings.evidence: The spec references hardcoded host-controller badge labels at `app/host/[sessionId]/page.tsx:1052-1054` and fallback `nextMs: 30_000` at `app/host/[sessionId]/page.tsx:1025`/`:1057`, but that file's contents are not included in the pack.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The actual code may only use those fallbacks for brand-new sessions and may already derive runtime behavior from stored config.
findings.what_would_confirm: Inspect the host controller's config resolution and add a test/import fixture for a stored 40s session and a missing-config legacy session.

findings.description: Failure sequence: in the table, the host changes the inline Brand dropdown and immediately clicks Re-download before the PUT and `refreshSessions()` complete; `onRedownload` builds the form from the stale row object, so the downloaded pack can use the old brand. The table implementation should make brand updates pending per row or update local session state before enabling dependent actions.
findings.evidence: Re-download reads `session.brandId` into the form at `app/host/page.tsx:168-170`, while brand changes are async and only refresh sessions after the PUT at `app/host/page.tsx:332-341`; the table spec does not mention disabling actions during this transition.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: This is only reachable if the inline selector allows immediate action before its async `onChange` resolves, which depends on `BrandSelector` behavior not shown in the pack.
findings.what_would_confirm: Exercise select-brand-then-re-download without waiting for refresh and inspect the generated event pack's `brand_id`.

findings.description: Failure sequence: the dashboard has multiple saved sessions with the same name/event date/game themes; the proposed table no longer shows Created or another unique discriminator, so a host can open, re-download, or delete the wrong row while the UI reports success. The current card layout shows Created, which is useful operational context when sessions accumulate.
findings.evidence: Current cards display `Created: {new Date(session.createdAt).toLocaleString()}` at `app/host/page.tsx:283-288`, but the proposed table columns are only Name, Event Date, Games, Brand, and Actions.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: If session names are guaranteed unique or the table adds sorting/created metadata elsewhere, the risk is reduced; the pack does not show such a guarantee.
findings.what_would_confirm: Create two sessions with the same display name and event date, then verify the table and delete confirmation let the host distinguish them.

findings.description: Failure sequence: implementation passes updated default tests and visual checks, but no test opens an existing 40s session; the hardcoded UI and fallback regressions above can ship despite the main acceptance concern. Add a legacy session fixture that asserts stored `revealConfig` controls host/guest labels and auto-advance.
findings.evidence: The spec testing plan verifies default countdown, challenge, extension, and dashboard actions, but it does not include a saved 40s session regression even though existing sessions are called out as a user concern.
findings.why_it_might_be_wrong: The full `npm run verify` may include coverage not described in the spec, but that is not evidenced in the pack.
findings.what_would_confirm: Find or add an E2E/unit fixture for a saved session with 13/27/33/40 timings and verify it still behaves that way after the default changes.

empty_categories: No duplicate auto-advance path was found in the reveal helper included in the pack.
No guest subscription or polling cleanup leak was found in the shown effects; intervals are cleared and the channel is unsubscribed on unmount.
No new database write, schema migration, or external API failure path is introduced by the timing spec itself.
